touches.ai_summary: Cold email sent to Miles Brown introducing AskElephant's call recording and coaching platform. No response received.
Follow-up cold email to Miles Brown highlighting ROI metrics for sales teams. No response received.
Cold call attempt to Miles Brown. Left voicemail introducing AskElephant and requesting a brief conversation about their sales coaching needs.
Cold email with case study from similar vertical SaaS company showing 23% improvement in win rates. No response received.
Cold call attempt to Miles Brown. Call went to voicemail, left message referencing previous case study email.

Initial qualification call with Miles Brown. Discussed VioletScope's current sales coaching challenges and lack of systematic call recording. Miles mentioned their team of ~25 sellers struggles with consistency and onboarding. Expressed interest in automated coaching insights. Rep qualified budget range and timeline.

questions_asked.question: What's driving your interest in call recording and coaching right now?
questions_asked.answer: We've grown quickly to 605 people and our sales team lacks consistency. New reps take too long to ramp and we don't have visibility into what's working.

questions_asked.question: How are you handling coaching today?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly ad-hoc. Sales managers listen to a few calls when they have time, but there's no systematic approach. Ethan Turner, our Sales Manager, has been pushing for something more scalable.

questions_asked.question: How does your platform handle automated coaching versus manual review?
questions_asked.answer: Our AI analyzes every call and surfaces key moments, objections, and best practices automatically. Managers can then dive deeper on flagged calls rather than listening to everything.

next_steps: Rep to send overview deck and pricing information
Miles to loop in Ethan Turner for technical perspective
Schedule discovery call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Miles with promised overview deck, pricing tiers, and ROI calculator. Also included links to two customer video testimonials from mid-market SaaS companies.

questions_asked.question: Can you share more detail on the unlimited automation tier versus basic?
questions_asked.answer: Unlimited automation includes custom coaching workflows, automated scorecards, and integration with your CRM for opportunity insights. Basic is call recording + AI summaries only.

touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message from Miles thanking rep for the materials and confirming Ethan Turner's availability for discovery call. Mentioned that Rowan Singh from their implementation team may also need to be involved for procurement requirements.
Email confirming discovery call schedule with Miles and Ethan. Rep also asked about involving Rowan Singh and any security/compliance requirements to address early.

questions_asked.question: Should we include Rowan Singh on the discovery call or bring them in later during technical evaluation?
questions_asked.answer: Let's start with just Ethan and me. We'll bring Rowan in once we're aligned on the use case.

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Miles and Ethan Turner. Deep dive into VioletScope's sales process, team structure (26 sellers across 3 segments), and specific coaching pain points. Ethan described challenges with remote team distributed across timezones. Discussed integration requirements with their existing Salesforce setup and Zoom. Rep demonstrated basic platform navigation.

questions_asked.question: We use Zoom for all our calls - how seamless is the integration?
questions_asked.answer: Fully automated. Once connected, every Zoom call is automatically recorded and processed. No manual uploads needed.

questions_asked.question: What does success look like for you in 6 months if you implement this?
questions_asked.answer: Faster ramp time for new reps, higher win rates on competitive deals, and Ethan's team spending less time on manual call review and more on strategic coaching.

questions_asked.question: Do you have conversation intelligence features or just recording?
questions_asked.answer: Full conversation intelligence - talk time ratios, question tracking, competitor mentions, objection patterns, and custom topic tracking based on your sales methodology.

next_steps: Schedule technical evaluation session
Rep to provide Salesforce integration documentation
Miles to confirm budget approval process and timeline

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with Salesforce integration guide and proposed agenda for technical evaluation. Rep also included security documentation proactively and asked about procurement timeline.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline from signature to full rollout?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers are live within 2 weeks. Setup is minimal - mainly SSO configuration and integration connections. Training typically takes 1-2 sessions.

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation session with Miles, Ethan, and (joined partway through) Rowan Singh. Walked through Salesforce sync, Zoom integration setup, and data security architecture. Rowan asked detailed questions about data retention, SOC2 compliance, and user provisioning. Ethan tested the coaching dashboard and AI summary quality with sample calls. Some concerns raised about customization limitations in automated workflows.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the AI coaching criteria to match our internal sales methodology?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, you can define custom scorecards and keywords. There are some limitations on the basic tier, but unlimited automation includes full customization.

questions_asked.question: What's your data retention policy and can we configure it?
questions_asked.answer: Default is 2 years, configurable down to 90 days if needed for compliance. All data encrypted at rest and in transit. We're SOC2 Type II certified.

questions_asked.question: Rowan, what are your key procurement requirements we should address?
questions_asked.answer: Standard vendor assessment - security questionnaire, SLA commitments, and our legal team will need to review the MSA. Timeline depends on contract value.

questions_asked.question: How does pricing scale if we add more seats later?
questions_asked.answer: You can add seats anytime at the same per-seat rate. We typically recommend starting with your core team and expanding after you see results.

objections: Workflow customization feels limited compared to what we might need long-term
next_steps: Rep to send security questionnaire and SLA documentation to Rowan
Schedule product demo focused on coaching workflows
Miles to confirm executive stakeholder for business case approval

touches.ai_summary: Full product demo with Miles, Ethan, and two other Sales Managers. Showcased automated coaching workflows, deal intelligence tied to conversations, and manager analytics dashboard. Demonstrated how AI flags high-risk deals based on conversation patterns. Team engaged positively with the interface. Some questions about reporting customization and whether dashboard metrics can export to their existing BI tool.

questions_asked.question: Can we export the conversation analytics into our Looker dashboards?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have a REST API and pre-built Looker blocks for common metrics. Custom exports also available via CSV or scheduled reports.

questions_asked.question: Which features are most valuable for your immediate needs versus nice-to-haves?
questions_asked.answer: Must-haves are call recording, AI summaries, and automated coaching scorecards. Deal intelligence is valuable but secondary. Ethan really wants the competitor tracking feature.

questions_asked.question: How accurate is the AI in identifying objections and next steps?
questions_asked.answer: Around 85-90% accuracy based on our benchmarks. It improves over time as it learns your sales language. Reps can flag incorrect summaries to improve the model.

objections: Reporting customization seems complex - would need support from your team to set up initially
next_steps: Rep to prepare proposal for 26 seats with unlimited automation tier
Miles to identify executive sponsor to present business case
Schedule proposal review call

touches.ai_summary: Brief proposal review call with Miles. Walked through pricing: $20,400 ACV for 26 seats on unlimited automation tier. Miles expressed concern about budget and asked about flexibility. Rep offered 25% discount to fit budget constraints. Miles appreciated the flexibility but mentioned he still needs executive approval and doesn't have a confirmed champion at the VP level yet.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on the pricing? Our approved budget is closer to $18K.
questions_asked.answer: Let me see what I can do. Given you're coming in at 26 seats, I can offer 25% off to get you to $20,400. That's the best I can do without involving my VP.

questions_asked.question: Who on the executive team will be approving this investment?
questions_asked.answer: That's still TBD. I have approval authority up to $15K, but anything above needs to go through our CFO or CRO. I'm working on building the business case now.

objections: Budget constraints - original pricing above approved amount
next_steps: Miles to present business case to executive team
Rep to send formal proposal document with discounted pricing
Schedule executive alignment meeting once stakeholder identified

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with Miles and Sarah Chen (CFO). Miles presented business case focusing on rep productivity and faster ramp time. Sarah asked detailed questions about ROI assumptions and contract terms. She was skeptical about projected win rate improvement and wanted to see customer references with similar profiles. Meeting tone was professional but cautious - Sarah clearly owns final decision and wasn't fully sold on urgency.

questions_asked.question: What's the basis for the 15% win rate improvement in your ROI model?
questions_asked.answer: Based on average across our mid-market customers in similar industries. We can connect you with 2-3 references who've seen comparable results.

questions_asked.question: What are the contract terms - can we do annual with an out-clause after 6 months if it's not working?
questions_asked.answer: Standard terms are annual commitment. We don't typically include early termination clauses, but we do offer a 30-day satisfaction guarantee where you can cancel for full refund if not satisfied with initial setup.

questions_asked.question: What would make you confident this is the right investment for VioletScope right now?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, I need to see proof this drives revenue impact, not just operational efficiency. References would help. Also want to understand implementation risk - we can't afford a prolonged rollout.

questions_asked.question: Are there smaller packages we could pilot with before committing all 26 seats?
questions_asked.answer: The discounted pricing is based on the 26-seat commitment. We could do a 10-seat pilot at standard pricing, but you'd lose the volume discount.

objections: ROI projections seem optimistic - wants proof from similar customers
Concerned about implementation risk and timeline
next_steps: Rep to provide 3 customer references with similar profiles
Rep to send implementation plan with timeline and resource requirements
Sarah to review with CRO before next discussion

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Miles and Rowan Singh to discuss contract terms and implementation plan. Rowan walked through their standard procurement process and flagged several MSA clauses that need modification. Miles pushed for additional discount or added services to strengthen the business case for Sarah. Rep held firm on pricing but offered extended onboarding support. Discussion of payment terms - VioletScope wants to pay quarterly, rep pushing for annual prepay.

questions_asked.question: Can we structure payment as quarterly installments instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Our standard terms are annual prepay, but I can potentially do semi-annual payments at the same discounted rate. Quarterly would require going back to standard pricing.

questions_asked.question: What does onboarding support include and can we get dedicated CSM resources?
questions_asked.answer: Onboarding includes implementation kickoff, integration setup, and 2 training sessions. Dedicated CSM is typically for customers above $50K ACV, but I can include quarterly business reviews as part of this deal.

questions_asked.question: Rowan, what are the main MSA issues we need to resolve?
questions_asked.answer: Indemnification caps, liability limits, and data processing addendum language. I'll send our redlines by end of week. Also need to add our standard security audit rights clause.

objections: Payment terms - strong preference for quarterly vs annual prepay
MSA redlines required before legal approval
next_steps: Rowan to send MSA redlines within 3 days
Rep to review with legal team and respond
Rep to confirm semi-annual payment option with finance
Miles to follow up with Sarah on customer references

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email from rep with customer reference contact information (3 mid-market SaaS companies), updated implementation timeline showing 10-day setup, and confirmation that semi-annual payments are approved. Also attached responses to Rowan's security questions and requested MSA redlines.

Brief exec check-in call with Miles to discuss contract status. Miles confirmed Sarah spoke with one reference and was more comfortable with the ROI case. However, legal review of MSA is taking longer than expected due to backlog. Miles mentioned there's no formal executive sponsor driving urgency internally - he's championing it but Sarah and CRO are focused on other priorities this quarter. Rep asked about timeline to close, Miles was noncommittal, saying 'likely next few weeks' but couldn't commit to specific date.

questions_asked.question: What's the realistic timeline to get legal review completed and contracts signed?
questions_asked.answer: Hard to say. Legal is backed up and this isn't top priority for them since it's under $25K. Probably 2-3 weeks, maybe longer.

questions_asked.question: Is there anything blocking us besides legal review at this point?
questions_asked.answer: Not really blocking, but Sarah hasn't fully committed. She's supportive in principle but wants to see how Q2 budget shakes out. The CRO hasn't weighed in at all yet.

next_steps: Miles to push legal review forward
Miles to sync with CRO on strategic alignment
Rep to follow up next week on contract status
